title: glebionis segetum
intro: Glebionis segetum, also known as corn marigold, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Glebionis segetum include its ability to reduce fever, promote digestion, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Glebionis segetum can be found in the form of tea, infusion, and tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
